laravel.validation.summary: Validates incoming HTTP request data using Laravel's validation rules.
laravel.validation.description: Comprehensive validation system for request data. Supports rules like required, string, integer, email, regex, between, max, min, etc. Includes custom validation, error messages, and request preparation.
tips: Use validated data instead of raw request input.
Chain validation methods for complex conditions.

php.solid.summary: Single Responsibility Principle - Keep classes focused on one responsibility.
php.solid.description: Design principle stating that a class should have only one reason to change. Each class should have a single, well-defined responsibility and do one thing well. Promotes modular, maintainable, and testable code architecture.
examples: class UserRepository { public function findById(int $id): User {} }
class EmailValidator { public function isValid(string $email): bool {} }
class OrderCalculator { public function calculateTotal(Order $order): float {} }
tips: Use interfaces to separate abstraction from implementation.
Extract helper methods when a method becomes too complex.

php.arrays.summary: Array manipulation techniques and best practices.
php.arrays.description: Essential PHP array operations for data processing. Includes array filtering, mapping, reducing, searching, and transforming operations to efficiently handle collections of data.
tips: Use array_key_exists for secure key checking.

symfony.routing.summary: RESTful routing patterns and best practices.
symfony.routing.description: Symfony's routing system for creating clean, RESTful URLs. Supports patterns, requirements, defaults, and advanced routing features for building scalable web applications.
rules: use named routes for URL generation
tips: Group routes with Route::group() for better organization.
Use route model binding for automatic Eloquent loading.

php.exceptions.summary: Robust exception handling and error management.
php.exceptions.description: Best practices for catching, handling, and logging exceptions in PHP. Includes try-catch blocks, custom exceptions, error levels, and graceful error recovery strategies.
rules: never let exceptions escape to API responses
tips: Use HttpException classes for HTTP-related errors.
Implement exception chaining for better context.
